prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on pyridine (C₅H₅N, 11 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken from the user-supplied pyridine.xyz as-is (single point, no re-optimization). Exact four-center integrals (density fitting not used). SCF converged in 10 cycles. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy = true. No warnings reported.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV (Koopmans-style orbital gap).
HOMO (index 20) = −7.2525992 eV
LUMO (index 21) = −1.1127860 eV

Additional frontier orbitals:
  HOMO−2 (index 18) = −8.0425195 eV
  HOMO−1 (index 19) = −7.4731156 eV
  LUMO+1 (index 22) = −0.6723154 eV
  LUMO+2 (index 23) = +1.1781069 eV

Koopmans descriptors:
  Vertical IP = 7.2525992 eV
  Vertical EA = 1.1127860 eV
  Electronegativity χ = 4.1826926 eV
  Chemical hardness η = 3.0699066 eV
  Chemical softness S = 0.32574281 eV⁻¹
  Electrophilicity index ω = 2.8494218 eV

Total electronic energy: −248.3782 Hartree (−6758.7152 eV). This energy zero is B3LYP/def2-TZVP-specific and is not directly comparable to energies from other methods or basis sets.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier pyridine.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Result JSON: pyridine_frontier_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json
